meta_title: For Freelancers: SaaS starter kit to Solve can't scale projects (Intermediate Checklist)
meta_description: Learn how to SaaS starter kit and overcome can't scale projects. A Intermediate-level Checklist using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: For Freelancers: SaaS Starter Kit to Solve Scaling Issues
content.seo_description: Overcome scaling bottlenecks with this intermediate SaaS starter kit checklist. Master Antigravity and Claude Code to deliver high-end projects faster.
content.answer_block: To stop struggling with scaling, you must transition from manual coding to an automated, component-driven SaaS starter kit. By leveraging Antigravity for architectural scaffolding and Claude Code for rapid implementation, you can reduce development time by 70%, ensuring repeatable quality and predictable delivery for every client project you undertake.
content.intro:
The midnight oil is burning, another deadline is looming, and your current project architecture is crumbling under the weight of manual configuration. You are trapped in a cycle of 'reinventing the wheel' for every new client, sacrificing your margins and your sanity to repetitive boilerplate setup.

But the chaos ends here. By adopting a hardened SaaS starter kit, you stop being a laborer and start being an architect. This guide bridges the gap between 'getting it done' and 'shipping professional-grade software' using the raw power of Antigravity and Claude Code.

steps.title: Standardize Your Auth and Database Layer
steps.description: Eliminate the 'login headache' by baking authentication and database schemas directly into your base template so you never configure Auth0 or Supabase from scratch again.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Scaffold a production-ready Next.js project with Supabase Auth, Row Level Security, and a pre-configured user profile table.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run --prompt 'Initialize Supabase auth with standard user profile boilerplate'

steps.title: Implement Modular UI Components
steps.description: Stop chasing styling bugs. Use a component library that ships with your starter kit to ensure consistency and speed across client dashboards.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a library of Shadcn UI components optimized for a SaaS dashboard layout, including sidebar, nav, and data tables.

steps.title: Automate API Route Scaffolding
steps.description: Standardize your API endpoints to handle errors, validation, and logging automatically, so you don't repeat the same boilerplate code for every CRUD operation.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a reusable API handler pattern for Next.js that includes Zod validation and structured error logging.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run --prompt 'Apply standard API error handling middleware to all routes'

steps.title: Integrate Stripe Billing Logic
steps.description: Payment integration is a major friction point. Pre-build your subscription logic so it's a 'plug-and-play' module for new clients.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Configure a Stripe checkout flow with webhook support for subscription status updates and user billing portal access.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run --prompt 'Integrate Stripe billing webhooks and subscription management flow'

steps.title: Deploy CI/CD Pipelines
steps.description: Ensure your project is always ready for production by automating the deployment pipeline, reducing manual 'git push' anxiety.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a GitHub Actions workflow for automated testing and deployment to Vercel upon merging to the main branch.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run --prompt 'Set up GitHub Actions for automated Vercel deployment'

pro_tips: Always store your 'Golden Template' in a private repository and use git submodules to pull updates into client projects.
Use Claude Code to write unit tests for your boilerplate before you even start the client-specific logic.
Standardize your environment variable naming across all projects to prevent configuration drift during deployments.
Adopt a 'feature flag' architecture within your starter kit to turn off unfinished client modules without breaking the build.
Keep your starter kit minimal; if you haven't used a library in three projects, strip it out to keep the bundle size lean.
content.next_steps: Clone your newly created starter kit into a private GitHub repository, invite a test user to verify the auth flow, and prepare a 15-minute demo to show your next potential client how fast you can launch their MVP.
content.conversion_block: Stop building from scratch and start scaling your freelance empire today. You have the tools, the blueprint, and the expertise. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
